rationale: The only change was employer names (re:cinq→Acme, Optilyz→Pinepoint, GISCollective→Northwind Labs), which carry no job-relevant signal. The score is identical (5/10), and both evaluations flag the same core concerns: basic German vs. C1 requirement, no UML/architecture modeling, and lack of client-facing/leadership evidence. The wording differences (added mentions of Linux, DLang, Python-adjacent ecosystems) read as ordinary run-to-run variation in how the model paraphrases the same resume, not as penalties or credits tied to the changed company names. Notably the variant still references GIS/GISCollective work despite the rename, confirming the substance is unchanged.
